judgment.summary: Cloudflare is a high-growth company with strong revenue expansion and excellent gross margins, but it is not yet profitable on an operating or net basis and carries significant debt.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported quarter (ending 2026-03-31) EPS was $0.25, beating the estimate of $0.23 by 7.0%. Next earnings date is 2026-07-30.
positives: Revenue growth of 33.5% YoY indicates strong market adoption.
Gross margin of 73.3% is best-in-class, demonstrating pricing power and efficient service delivery.
Free cash flow of $755.00M TTM shows the company is generating cash from operations.
Analyst consensus is a buy with a mean target price of $234.18, suggesting upside potential.
Current ratio of 1.96 and quick ratio of 1.90 indicate solid short-term liquidity.
negatives: Operating margin of -9.7% and profit margin of -3.7% show the company is not yet profitable.
Return on equity of -5.9% and return on assets of -2.7% highlight poor profitability relative to capital.
Debt/Equity ratio of 230.86 is very high, indicating significant financial leverage.
Forward P/E of 137.78 suggests the stock is trading at a premium valuation, priced for substantial future growth.
Beta of 1.67 indicates higher volatility than the broader market.

news.paragraph: Cloudflare (NET) recently reported first-quarter results that exceeded expectations, although the market's reaction was described as mixed. While the company's financial performance was positive, other news includes a strike by Net-a-Porter warehouse workers and a significant investment in Broadstone Net Lease, which may influence trader sentiment. No other immediate catalysts like product launches or regulatory actions are apparent from the provided headlines.

headlines.title: Cloudflare (NET) Q1 Results Beat Expectations, But Market Reaction Tells A Mixed Story
